stories.title: White Sox Tied for First in AL Central — The Rebuild Has Arrived
stories.angle: The Chicago White Sox beat the Los Angeles Dodgers 6-4 Sunday (June 14) and are now tied with Cleveland for first place in the AL Central. Colson Montgomery, Sam Antonacci, and Chase Meidroth all homered in a six-run sixth inning. Chicago won its first series against the Dodgers since 2014. From 41-121 in 2024 to first place in June 2026 — the most surprising story in baseball.

article.headline: The White Sox Are Tied for First Place — The Rebuild Has Arrived

stories.title: Rockies 23, Athletics 9 — Franchise Record for Runs Scored
stories.angle: The Colorado Rockies set a franchise record for runs in a game, beating the Las Vegas Athletics 23-9 on Sunday (June 14) at Las Vegas Ballpark. Willi Castro drove in 7 runs including a grand slam off Scott Barlow. Hunter Goodman went 5-for-5 with 2 HR and 4 RBI. Kyle Karros added 4 hits. Colorado hit 6 home runs total and put up 24 hits.

article.headline: Rockies 23, Athletics 9 — Colorado Sets Franchise Record for Runs in a Game

stories.title: Braves Lose Third Straight — Mets 8, Braves 1 Sunday, Acuña Watch
stories.angle: The Atlanta Braves dropped their third straight game since Ronald Acuña Jr. went on the IL with a hamstring strain, falling to the Mets 8-1 on Sunday (June 14). Atlanta still holds the best record in baseball at approximately 45-24 and maintains a nine-game NL East lead, but the three-game skid without their star is a concern entering the week.

article.headline: Braves Lose Three Straight Without Acuña — The Best Team in Baseball Is Showing Vulnerability

stories.title: Schwarber-Alvarez Still Tied at 24 HR — Derby Countdown Begins
stories.angle: The Phillies fell to the Brewers 4-0 on Sunday (June 14) with Schwarber held without a home run. The Astros also lost to the Royals 4-0, keeping Alvarez also idle. Both remain tied at 24 HR heading into Monday. The Home Run Derby is July 13 at Citizens Bank Park — Schwarber's home ballpark — and Bryce Harper is also considering participating.

article.headline: Schwarber and Alvarez Both Go Cold Sunday — Still Tied at 24, Derby Countdown Begins

stories.title: Sunday Night Roundup — Rangers Win SNB, Tigers Postponed, Cubs Split
stories.angle: Sunday Night Baseball: Rangers 6, Red Sox 4 — Nathan Eovaldi threw 7 innings and Wyatt Langford homered. The Tigers-Guardians game was postponed due to weather, delaying Casey Mize's return to Wednesday in Houston. The Giants beat the Cubs 5-1, completing a series split. All-Star Game voting runs through June 25; game is July 14 in Philadelphia.

article.headline: Sunday Baseball Roundup: Rangers Win SNB, Cubs Split, Mize's Return Delayed Again
